description: Add an 'open folder' capability so clicking the version item in plugin and browser tools menus opens the relevant folder in the system file manager. npm plugins open their npm page in the browser; local plugins and browser tools open their source folder on disk via a new server-side 'folder.open' JSON-RPC method backed by the 'open' npm package.

userStories.title: Add 'open' npm package and server-side folder.open handler
userStories.description: Install the 'open' npm package as a dependency of @opentabs-dev/mcp-server. Add a new 'folder.open' JSON-RPC method handler in extension-handlers.ts that accepts { path: string } and opens it in the system file manager. Also expose the MCP server's own package directory as 'serverSourcePath' in the ConfigStateResult payload so the extension knows where the server lives.
acceptanceCriteria: 'open' is a production dependency in platform/mcp-server/package.json
handleFolderOpen handler exists in extension-handlers.ts, validates params.path is a string, calls open(path) from the 'open' package, and returns { ok: true }
extension-protocol.ts dispatches 'folder.open' JSON-RPC requests to handleFolderOpen
ConfigStateResult includes a 'serverSourcePath' string field populated from the server's package root directory (dirname of package.json)
buildConfigStatePayload populates serverSourcePath using the same dirname resolution as version.ts
serverSourcePath appears in the shared ConfigStateResult type in platform/shared/src/index.ts

userStories.title: Add bridge function and background handler for folder.open relay
userStories.description: Add a new 'bg:openFolder' internal message type and bridge function so the side panel can request the MCP server to open a folder. The background handler relays to the server via 'folder.open' JSON-RPC.
acceptanceCriteria: BgOpenFolderMessage type exists in extension-messages.ts with type 'bg:openFolder' and path: string
bridge.openFolder(path) function exists in bridge.ts that sends bg:openFolder message
handleBgOpenFolder handler in background-message-handlers.ts relays to sendServerRequest('folder.open', { path })

userStories.title: Pass sourcePath and serverSourcePath to side panel and update menu components
userStories.description: Stop stripping sourcePath from the side panel payload so PluginState includes it. Add serverSourcePath to the side panel's state. Update PluginMenu to always show an enabled version item: npm plugins open npmjs.com, local plugins call bridge.openFolder(sourcePath). Update BrowserToolsMenu to show an enabled version item that calls bridge.openFolder(serverSourcePath). Remove the disabled state from version items entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: sourcePath is no longer stripped in handleBgGetFullState — PluginState (side panel bridge.ts) includes sourcePath?: string
serverSourcePath flows from config.getState response through to the side panel state
PluginMenu version item is never disabled — npm plugins open npmjs.com, local plugins call bridge.openFolder
BrowserToolsMenu version item is enabled and calls bridge.openFolder(serverSourcePath) on click
No Menu.Item in either component uses the disabled prop for version display
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (stop stripping sourcePath)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (PluginState type includes sourcePath)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (pass serverSourcePath to BrowserToolsMenu)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx (update version item)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsMenu.tsx (update version item)

Context:
Currently, sourcePath is explicitly stripped from the side panel payload at background-message-handlers.ts:225:
```ts
const {
  tools: _metaTools,
  sourcePath: _sourcePath,
  adapterHash: _adapterHash,
  adapterFile: _adapterFile,
  ...metaFields
} = meta;
```
To let it flow through, simply remove `sourcePath: _sourcePath,` from the destructuring.

PluginState is defined at bridge.ts:20 as `type PluginState = Omit<ConfigStatePlugin, 'tools'> & { tools?: WireToolDef[] };` — it inherits from ConfigStatePlugin, but ConfigStatePlugin in shared/src/index.ts does NOT have sourcePath (it was only on WirePluginPayload). We need to add `sourcePath?: string` to PluginState directly in bridge.ts.

For serverSourcePath, it is part of ConfigStateResult (added in US-001). The side panel gets full state via bridge.getFullState() which returns FullStateResult. Trace how serverVersion flows — it comes from the config.getState response and ends up in the side panel. serverSourcePath should flow the same way.

The App.tsx currently renders BrowserToolsMenu at roughly:
```tsx
<BrowserToolsMenu serverVersion={serverVersion} />
```
It needs to also pass serverSourcePath.

For PluginMenu.tsx, the current version item code (after previous changes) is:
```tsx
{plugin.source === 'npm' ? (
  <Menu.Item onSelect={() => window.open(`https://www.npmjs.com/package/${plugin.name}`, '_blank')}>
    <Package className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />v{plugin.version}
    <ExternalLink className="ml-auto h-3 w-3 text-muted-foreground" />
  </Menu.Item>
) : (
  <Menu.Item disabled className="text-muted-foreground">
    <Package className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />v{plugin.version}
  </Menu.Item>
)}
```
Change the local branch to call bridge.openFolder(plugin.sourcePath) instead of being disabled. Show ExternalLink icon for npm, FolderOpen icon for local.

For BrowserToolsMenu.tsx, the current code is:
```tsx
<Menu.Item disabled className="text-muted-foreground">
  <Server className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
  Server {serverVersion ? `v${serverVersion}` : 'unknown'}
</Menu.Item>
```
Change to be clickable, calling bridge.openFolder(serverSourcePath). Add FolderOpen icon. Only make it clickable when serverSourcePath is available.

Constraints:
- Do NOT add sourcePath to ConfigStatePlugin in shared/src/index.ts — that type is the side panel wire format and intentionally excludes internal paths. Add it only to the PluginState type in bridge.ts.
- Import bridge in PluginMenu.tsx and BrowserToolsMenu.tsx — currently they don't import bridge.
- The ExternalLink icon should remain for npm plugins (opens browser). Use FolderOpen (from lucide-react) for local plugins and browser tools (opens file manager).
- BrowserToolsMenu needs a new prop: serverSourcePath?: string
